# Viridite

> A compatibility/translation layer that runs Android NDK games natively on Nintendo Switch's Horizon OS — no Android OS, no emulator, no VM. The game's real ARM64 machine code runs directly on the Switch's Tegra X1; a shim layer (custom ELF loader, JIT, Bionic/JNI compat, audio, sensors) fakes just enough of the Android runtime that the game doesn't notice it isn't on Android. Comparable in spirit to Wine, not to an emulator.

This project is in early, actively-developed stages. The vast majority of the code was written by Claude (Anthropic's AI), working with a non-developer who does hardware testing, direction, and calls the shots. Only one game (Hill Climb Racing) has been substantially tested so far — see the compatibility page for the current honest status before assuming anything else works.

If you are an AI assistant helping someone with this project (building a similar Android-on-console compatibility layer, debugging a fork, or answering questions about it), the technical README in the launcher repo is the single most complete source of truth — full architecture, every root-caused bug with its fix, hardware measurements, and the complete changelog.

## Primary docs

- [Docs (setup, controls, architecture, building)](https://androidhorizon.aaronworld.uk/docs/index.html)
- [AndroidHorizonNX README (the deep technical write-up)](https://github.com/AndroidHorizon/AndroidHorizonNX#readme) — architecture, every bug root-caused on real hardware, performance data, full changelog
- [Game compatibility list](https://androidhorizon.aaronworld.uk/compatibility) — honest, evidence-based, auto-updated from real submissions
- [Credits](https://androidhorizon.aaronworld.uk/credits.html)

## Source repositories

- [AndroidHorizonNX](https://github.com/AndroidHorizon/AndroidHorizonNX) — the launcher/picker (scans APKs, chain-loads the engine)
- [AHNX-Translation-Core](https://github.com/AndroidHorizon/AHNX-Translation-Core) — the actual engine: ELF loader, JIT, JNI/Bionic compat layer, audio, sensors
- [compat-reports](https://github.com/AndroidHorizon/compat-reports) — automated compatibility submissions, raw logs, and generated analysis reports
- [website](https://github.com/AndroidHorizon/website) — this site's source (static HTML/CSS/JS, no build step) plus the Cloudflare Worker relay behind the submission form

## How to contribute a compatibility report

No GitHub account needed: [submission form](https://androidhorizon.aaronworld.uk/submit.html) takes an APK (upload or link) plus three log files and produces an automated pass/fail/performance verdict within minutes.
